---
title: "Audit a derived map"
subtitle: "Recover a chained pipeline from the `.nii.gz` alone."
jupyter: python3
sidebar: cookbook
---

## Goal

You receive a `.nii.gz` with no producer, no sidecar, no Slack thread.
Recover *what produced it, from what input, with what mask, through
what chain of operations* from the file's bytes alone.

## Code

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
import sys
import tempfile

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path.cwd().parents[1] / "src"))

import nibabel as nib
import neuroim as ni

repo = Path.cwd().parents[1] if Path.cwd().name == "cookbook" else Path.cwd()
bold = ni.read_image(str(repo / "golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_bold.nii.gz"))
mask = ni.read_image(str(repo / "golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_mask.nii.gz"), type="vol")

# Producer: a two-stage pipeline — resample, then temporal SNR.
template4 = mask.space.add_dim(size=bold.shape[-1])
resampled = ni.resample_vec(bold, template4, interpolation=1)
tsnr = resampled.temporal_snr(mask=mask)

out = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp()) / "tsnr_template.nii.gz"
nib.save(tsnr.to_nibabel(), str(out))

# Auditor: a fresh process with only the path.
audited = ni.read_image(str(out))
audited.provenance.method_name
```

The `method_name` is **chained** — it names both pipeline stages, in
order, with the resample's parameters folded in:
`resample_vec(order=1,source=…,target=…)+temporal_snr`.

## What's guaranteed

- `resample_vec` records source/target space hashes and the
  interpolation order in its Receipt.
- `temporal_snr` *merges* the upstream Receipt rather than discarding
  it — the chain prefix is preserved across the reduction.
- The chained Receipt embeds on `to_nibabel()` and rehydrates on
  `read_image()`.

## What survives the file

```{python}
p = audited.provenance
(p.method_name, p.input_space_hash[:10], p.mask_hash[:10])
```

The terminal `input_space_hash` is the template frame; the
`mask_hash` is the mask used at the reduction; the `method_name`
carries the lineage. An auditor can confirm the source and mask by
recomputing their hashes if those files are still around.

## Trapdoors

- A pipeline step that returns a bare `ndarray` breaks the chain — the
  next step has no upstream Receipt to merge. Keep derived values in
  their typed carriers until the final write. Backed by
  [scenario S14](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/14_temporal_slice_provenance/REPORT.md)
  (time-axis slicing as silent provenance loss).
- `ni.write_vol` does not yet embed; use `to_nibabel().save(...)`
  ([S19 PAIN-4](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/19_group_mean_template_tsnr/REPORT.md)).

## See also

- [Concept: Provenance](../concepts/provenance.qmd) — chaining semantics.
- [Spec: Receipt NIfTI extension](../spec/receipt-nifti-extension.html)
- Scenarios [S13](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/13_provenance_audit/REPORT.md), [S09](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/09_native_to_template_provenance/REPORT.md)
